source_jobs.title: Registered Behavior Technician (RBT)
source_jobs.description:
About us

In an industry often challenged by high burnout rates, unsupportive work environments, and underwhelming compensation, Tellos stands out as an exception. We recognize these industry challenges and have committed to creating a different kind of workplace. At Tellos, we prioritize the well-being and professional satisfaction of our team members. We understand that the success of our employees directly translates to the quality of care we provide. Join us for a career experience that's as rewarding as it is impactful.

Job Description:

As a Registered Behavior Technician (RBT), you will be responsible for implementing individualized treatment plans under the supervision of a Board Certified Behavior Analyst (BCBA). You will play a crucial role in helping our clients develop essential skills and improve their overall quality of life.

Responsibilities:

 - Implement individualized ABA treatment plans designed by a BCBA

 - Collect and record data on client progress and behavior

 - Assist with functional behavior assessments and skill assessments

 - Provide 1:1 direct ABA therapy to clients in a home setting

 - Collaborate with a multidisciplinary team, including BCBAs, Speech Therapists, and Occupational Therapists

 - Attend team meetings and participate in ongoing training and professional development opportunities

 - Maintain client confidentiality and adhere to ethical guidelines established by the Behavior Analyst Certification Board (BACB)

Requirements:

 - Registered Behavior Technician Certification or Behavior Technician strongly preferred.

 - Minimum of 6 months experience working with children with autism or other developmental disabilities (preferred)

 - Valid driver's license and reliable transportation

 - Ability to pass a background check

 - Compassionate, patient, and dedicated to helping improve the lives of clients

 - High school diploma or equivalent

Benefits:

 - Truly caring and nurturing culture

 - Highest hourly pay than market

 - Multiple opportunities for career advancement

 - Supportive and collaborative work environment

 - Paid training

Join our team and be part of a caring and supportive community that is dedicated to helping you succeed in your ABA career. Join us and start making a difference in the lives of children and families!

We believe in hiring people, not resumes. If you’re excited by Tellos’s mission but unsure if you meet every requirement, we still encourage you to apply, we’d love to learn what you’d bring to the team.

source_jobs.title: Spanish-Speaking BCBA (1099), Full Admin Support & Autonomy – South Carolina Based
source_jobs.description:
You became a Board Certified Behavioral Analyst to help kiddos and their families thrive, grow, and accomplish. But the industry has made that challenging for you. With high case loads, long commutes, pressure on minimum hours, and staff turnover, it's hard to accomplish your goals.


At Tellos, our mission is to help you be the BCBA you want to be.


We do this through lower caseloads, stable RBT staff, Telehealth, and a platform that gives you autonomy to manage your cases the way you know is best. By Providing BCBAs with everything they need to go independent, from finding clients to credentialing, scheduling, billing and recruiting, we never stop supporting you and your goals.

What the role entails!

 - Conduct comprehensive assessments, including functional behavior assessments (FBAs) and skill assessments

 - Develop and monitor individualized ABA treatment plans for clients, setting measurable goals and objectives

 - Supervise, train, and support RBTs in the implementation of treatment plans

 - Evaluate client progress and adjust treatment plans as necessary

 - Attend team meetings and participate in ongoing training and professional development opportunities

 - Maintain client confidentiality and adhere to ethical guidelines established by the Behavior Analyst Certification Board (BACB)

 - Helping our staff understand what features are most important to help parents and providers in offering the best quality of care

 - Communicate with our team on billing and insurance to meet payor requirements and submission deadlines

Qualifications

 - Board Certified Behavioral Analyst Certification

 - Masters Degree

 - Must live in South Carolina

 - Fluent in Spanish (required) – able to effectively communicate with Spanish-speaking clients and families

 - Strong leadership skills

 - Passionate about delivering high-quality, personalized care.

 - Driven, with a strong desire to establish an independent practice.

 - Strong communication skills

How It Work

 - Chat With Us: talk to our clinical director about your goals

 - Get Setup: we’ll help you setup your practice, preparing everything you need for success

 - Go Live: Once everything is setup, you can start seeing clients in few weeks.

Why work with us?

We help you go independent allowing you to have the autonomy and flexibility you need to deliver world-class care to the families and kiddos who need you most, and earn even more as independent practitioner.

 - Purpose-driven: we believe that providers must have fulfilling and meaningful career to offer their best.

 - Highest standard of care- BCBAs are overworked and underpaid, we want to do the opposite.

 - Empathy - the families come first but the precursor to that is the happiness of the providers, so we’ve a provider first culture.

 - You’ll be in the driver seat, running your own practice without any external influence on how to run your business.

Earning Range: Up to $125,000+ per year

We believe in hiring people, not resumes. If you’re excited by Tellos’s mission but unsure if you meet every requirement, we still encourage you to apply, we’d love to learn what you’d bring to the team.

source_jobs.title: Independent BCBA (1099), Full Admin Support & Autonomy, South Carolina Based
source_jobs.description:
Job Title: Independent BCBA (1099), Full Admin Support & Autonomy, South Carolina

Location: Onsite

Job Type: 1099 Independent Contractor

Earning Potential: up to $150,000+ per year (Based on caseload volume)

The Freedom to Focus on Clinical Care (We Handle the Rest)

Are you a BCBA tired of burnout, impossible caseloads, and drowning in administrative red tape? At Tellos, we believe that to provide the best clinical outcomes for children, providers need autonomy, support, and a healthy work-life balance.

We are looking for Independent Contractor (1099) BCBAs located in South Carolina who want to take control of their career.

How It Works

Think of this as "plug-and-play" independence. You act as the clinical leader, determining the best course of treatment for your clients. We act as your backend operations team.

You Focus On:

 - Clinical decisions and quality of care.

 - Determining your own schedule.

 - Deciding if a case requires in-person support or if telehealth is clinically appropriate.

 - Taking on as many (or as few) clients as fits your lifestyle.

We Handle:

 - RBT Staffing: We handle the sourcing, vetting, payroll, and retention perks; you make the final decision on who joins your team to ensure the right clinical fit.

 - The Paperwork: We handle credentialing, authorizations, billing, and claims submissions.

 - Client Acquisition: We manage the referral pipeline and intake process, connecting you directly with families seeking services so you don’t have to worry about marketing or outreach.

Key Responsibilities

 - Lead Clinically: Conduct robust functional behavior assessments (FBAs) and skill-based assessments to design individualized, evidence-based treatment plans that target meaningful outcomes.

 - Drive Treatment Fidelity: Provide competency-based supervision and mentorship to RBTs, ensuring protocols are implemented with high fidelity and clinical rigor.

 - Data-Based Decision Making: Utilize visual data analysis to monitor client progress, modifying instructional protocols and behavior intervention plans (BIPs) in real-time to ensure continuous growth.

 - Documentation & Medical Necessity: Collaborate with the Tellos admin team to ensure all clinical documentation clearly demonstrates medical necessity, meeting payer standards without compromising clinical depth.

 - Facilitate Caregiver Partnership: Partner with families through consistent collaboration and parent coaching to ensure skills generalize to the home environment, driving sustainable, long-term outcomes.

 - Ethical Practice: Uphold the highest standards of professional practice and client dignity in accordance with the BACB Ethics Code for Behavior Analysts.

Why Partner with Tellos?

 - Total Caseload Control: You decide your volume. Whether you want a full-time caseload or just a few clients to supplement your income, you are in the driver's seat.

 - Telehealth Flexibility: We support a telehealth model where clinically appropriate, giving you the ability to work from home as you see fit.

 - No Admin Burnout: Being independent doesn’t mean you have to handle insurance on your own. We have a dedicated team to handle the billing, denials, and authorization processes.

 - Recruitment Support: We manage the sourcing and first-round interviews so you don’t have to sift through resumes. We present you with vetted candidates for your final approval and provide the backend support systems you need to build a stable team that sticks with you.

Qualifications

 - Active Board Certified Behavior Analyst (BCBA) certification.

 - Active South Carolina LBA strongly preferred

 - Master’s Degree in Applied Behavior Analysis, Psychology, or related field.

 - Strong desire for autonomy and the discipline to manage an independent schedule.

 - Comfortable using technology for telehealth supervision and digital data collection.

Ready to define your own practice?

Step 1: Chat with us about your clinical philosophy and income goals.

Step

source_jobs.title: Child Development Specialist - $22–$28/hr | Paid Training - Hatteras, NC
source_jobs.description:
Child Development Specialist | Paid Training - Hatteras, NC

About Tellos
At Tellos, we know the work you do as an RBT takes heart, patience, and dedication. In an industry where burnout and lack of support are common, we’ve made it our mission to do things differently. Our BCBAs and leadership team truly care about your growth, well-being, and happiness at work. When you join Tellos, you join a team that values you as a person — not just as a provider.

What You’ll Do
As an RBT, you’ll help children with autism build essential skills and confidence through Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA). You’ll work closely with a BCBA who provides consistent guidance and mentorship while you deliver 1:1 therapy in the home or community setting. You’ll also track client progress, assist with behavior and skill assessments, and collaborate with a team that celebrates every milestone — big or small.

What We’re Looking For

 - Someone we can train the next 30 days to 60 days to be an RBT

 - An individual with experience supporting children, particularly those with special needs.

 - Compassionate, patient, and dedicated to helping kids grow

 - Reliable transportation and a valid driver’s license

 - High school diploma or equivalent

Why You’ll Love Working at Tellos

 - Competitive pay: $22–$28 per hour

 - Supportive BCBAs who invest in your growth and success

 - Paid training and ongoing professional development

 - Opportunities for career advancement within a growing ABA team

 - A genuinely caring and collaborative culture with low turnover

Join Our Team
If you’re passionate about making a difference and want to be part of a team that supports you every step of the way, Tellos is the place for you. Even if you don’t meet every requirement, we’d still love to hear your story. Apply today and start a career where you can grow, feel valued, and make an impact every day.

Work Location: In person

We believe in hiring people, not resumes. If you’re excited by Tellos’s mission but unsure if you meet every requirement, we still encourage you to apply, we’d love to learn what you’d bring to the team.

We are looking for Independent Contractor (1099) BCBAs located in New Hampshire who want to take control of their career.

How It Works

Think of this as "plug-and-play" independence. You act as the clinical leader, determining the best course of treatment for your clients. We act as your backend operations team.

You Focus On:

 - Clinical decisions and quality of care.

 - Determining your own schedule.

 - Deciding if a case requires in-person support or if telehealth is clinically appropriate.

 - Taking on as many (or as few) clients as fits your lifestyle.

We Handle:

 - RBT Staffing: We handle the sourcing, vetting, payroll, and retention perks; you make the final decision on who joins your team to ensure the right clinical fit.

 - The Paperwork: We handle credentialing, authorizations, billing, and claims submissions.

 - Client Acquisition: We manage the referral pipeline and intake process, connecting you directly with families seeking services so you don’t have to worry about marketing or outreach.

Key Responsibilities

 - Lead Clinically: Conduct robust functional behavior assessments (FBAs) and skill-based assessments to design individualized, evidence-based treatment plans that target meaningful outcomes.

 - Drive Treatment Fidelity: Provide competency-based supervision and mentorship to RBTs, ensuring protocols are implemented with high fidelity and clinical rigor.

 - Data-Based Decision Making: Utilize visual data analysis to monitor client progress, modifying instructional protocols and behavior intervention plans (BIPs) in real-time to ensure continuous growth.

 - Documentation & Medical Necessity: Collaborate with the Tellos admin team to ensure all clinical documentation clearly demonstrates medical necessity, meeting payer standards without compromising clinical depth.

 - Facilitate Caregiver Partnership: Partner with families through consistent collaboration and parent coaching to ensure skills generalize to the home environment, driving sustainable, long-term outcomes.

 - Ethical Practice: Uphold the highest standards of professional practice and client dignity in accordance with the BACB Ethics Code for Behavior Analysts.

Why Partner with Tellos?

 - Total Caseload Control: You decide your volume. Whether you want a full-time caseload or just a few clients to supplement your income, you are in the driver's seat.

 - Telehealth Flexibility: We support a telehealth model where clinically appropriate, giving you the ability to work from home as you see fit.

 - No Admin Burnout: Being independent doesn’t mean you have to handle insurance on your own. We have a dedicated team to handle the billing, denials, and authorization processes.

 - Recruitment Support: We manage the sourcing and first-round interviews so you don’t have to sift through resumes. We present you with vetted candidates for your final approval and provide the backend support systems you need to build a stable team that sticks with you.

Qualifications

 - Active Board Certified Behavior Analyst (BCBA) certification.

 - Active South Carolina LBA strongly preferred

 - Residence in CA, OR, ID, WA, UT, AZ preferred

 - Master’s Degree in Applied Behavior Analysis, Psychology, or related field.

 - Strong desire for autonomy and the discipline to manage an independent schedule.

 - Comfortable using technology for telehealth supervision and digital data collection.

Ready to define your own practice?

Step 1: Chat with us abou

source_jobs.title: Clinical Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
THIS ROLE REQUIRES MINIMUM 2 YEARS OF DIRECT ABA THERAPY RECRUITING EXPERIENCE (RBT + BCBA HIRING).



IF YOU DO NOT HAVE ABA RECRUITING EXPERIENCE, PLEASE DO NOT APPLY.




ABOUT TELLOS

Most companies in autism care get it wrong- burnout, bureaucracy, and broken promises.

We’re building the opposite.

We help BCBAs launch and run their own independent ABA practices, and we support RBTs with fair pay, respect, and real backup. Families trust us with their kids. Providers trust us with their careers. We take both seriously.

At Tellos, recruiting isn’t admin - it’s impact. How fast we hire is how fast a child gets therapy.

We’re looking for someone who truly understands ABA recruiting - what makes a strong RBT, what signals a high-caliber BCBA, and why every hire matters. A recruiter is a candidate’s first experience of our culture, and when you show up warm, clear, and human, it tells them everything about who we are.

If you want to own your lane, drive a high-output recruiting engine, and end each week knowing kids started services because of your work - not just because “a req was filled” - you’ll thrive here.


WHAT YOU’LL OWN

You won’t be “helping HR.” You will own outcomes in these areas:

What You’ll Own

 - Full-cycle recruiting for RBTs and BCBAs: Own the entire pipeline end-to-end - from posting to sourcing to screening to offer. Use LinkedIn, Indeed, referrals, Facebook groups, and outbound outreach to build strong pipelines and keep every high-quality candidate moving.

 - Partnership with BCBAs & Ops to keep cases staffed: Understand real case needs (schedule, location, clinical profile), re-staff quickly when gaps appear, add hours to existing RBT schedules, and keep kids from waiting weeks for therapy.

 - Keep the System Clean & Visible :Maintain an accurate, real-time recruitment metrics and clean dashboard so we always know: who’s in process, how long they’ve been there, weekly interview volume, offers, and time-to-hire. Provide instant visibility to leadership, no guessing, no manual digging.

What You’ll Need

 - ABA recruiting Experience: 2+ years hiring RBTs/BCBAs in ABA; skilled with job boards, ATS systems (Ashby), resume search, and outbound sourcing; strong written and verbal communication.

 - High-output, high-ownership mindset: You thrive in a fast-moving environment, adapt quickly when priorities shift, and when pipelines slow, your first instinct is “What can I try today?” not “Let’s wait and see".
   
   Why You’ll Love Working Here
   
   A mission worth your career
   
   We’re building the most clinician-centered ABA company in the country—no private-equity pressure, no burnout factory, no empty slogans. Every hire you make directly fuels a BCBA’s independence and a child’s chance to start therapy sooner.
   
   Extreme ownership, zero bureaucracy
   
   We move fast. You’ll own your lane end-to-end, without layers of approvals or “HR politics.” When you see a better way to recruit, you implement it. And the results are visible within days, not quarters.
   
   Your work compounds in real lives
   
   When you staff an RBT or bring in a great BCBA, parents get help faster. Therapists earn more and stay longer. Practices grow sustainably. You see the impact of your work in hours, not hypotheticals.
   
   A once-in-a-career growth opportunity
   
   Tellos is scaling nationwide. As volume grows, you grow. You’ll build systems, pipelines, playbooks, and infrastructure that you rarely get access to at later-stage companies. If you want a trajectory toward Senior Recruiter → Recruiting Lead → Talent Acquisition Manager, this is that path.
   
   A culture that actually cares
   
   Not in a vague HR way. In a “leaders know your work, your contribution, your voice, and your impact” way. We hire adults, trust them to operate like owners, and back them every step of the way.
   
   This Role Is for You If…
   
   - You can’t stomach seeing great candidates sit untouched. When you see talent, you

source_jobs.title: Registered Behavior Technician (RBT)
source_jobs.description:
About us

In an industry often challenged by high burnout rates, unsupportive work environments, and underwhelming compensation, Tellos stands out as an exception. We recognize these industry challenges and have committed to creating a different kind of workplace. At Tellos, we prioritize the well-being and professional satisfaction of our team members. We understand that the success of our employees directly translates to the quality of care we provide. Join us for a career experience that's as rewarding as it is impactful.

Job Description:

As a Registered Behavior Technician (RBT), you will be responsible for implementing individualized treatment plans under the supervision of a Board Certified Behavior Analyst (BCBA). You will play a crucial role in helping our clients develop essential skills and improve their overall quality of life.

Responsibilities:

 - Implement individualized ABA treatment plans designed by a BCBA

 - Collect and record data on client progress and behavior

 - Assist with functional behavior assessments and skill assessments

 - Provide 1:1 direct ABA therapy to clients in a home setting

 - Collaborate with a multidisciplinary team, including BCBAs, Speech Therapists, and Occupational Therapists

 - Attend team meetings and participate in ongoing training and professional development opportunities

 - Maintain client confidentiality and adhere to ethical guidelines established by the Behavior Analyst Certification Board (BACB)

Requirements:

 - Registered Behavior Technician Certification or Behavior Technician strongly preferred.

 - Minimum of 6 months experience working with children with autism or other developmental disabilities (preferred)

 - Valid driver's license and reliable transportation

 - Ability to pass a background check

 - Compassionate, patient, and dedicated to helping improve the lives of clients

 - High school diploma or equivalent

Benefits:

 - Truly caring and nurturing culture

 - Highest hourly pay than market

 - Multiple opportunities for career advancement

 - Supportive and collaborative work environment

 - Paid training

Join our team and be part of a caring and supportive community that is dedicated to helping you succeed in your ABA career. Join us and start making a difference in the lives of children and families!

We believe in hiring people, not resumes. If you’re excited by Tellos’s mission but unsure if you meet every requirement, we still encourage you to apply, we’d love to learn what you’d bring to the team.

source_jobs.title: Remote Independent BCBA (1099), Full Admin Support & Autonomy, Georgia Based
source_jobs.description:

Earning Potential: up to $150,000+ per year (Based on caseload volume)

notes: Tellos is a healthcare organization focused on autism care, with a strong emphasis on clinical care and provider well-being. The job postings suggest a high volume of hiring for Registered Behavior Technicians (RBTs) and Board Certified Behavior Analysts (BCBAs), with some administrative and recruiting roles. The organization appears to prioritize remote work and flexibility.
